item.title: Ready or Not: Boiling Point DLC is out now with three new maps
item.content:encoded:
Having teased their new DLC expansion last week, Void Interactive has now fully revealed and released the Ready or Not: Boiling Point DLC, bringing three new maps, more cosmetics, and some free guns and updates to the game.

The city of Los Sueños faces an existential crisis through Boiling Point’s three levels, as a gruesome terror attack leads to mass unrest and protest, and even a push to topple the local government.

There’s three levels in the Boiling Point DLC, which is priced at $9.99 / £7.99:

  • ‘No Good Deed’
  • ‘All Gods Burn’
  • ‘A New America’

Owning the DLC and hosting a multiplayer lobby will allow non-DLC owners to play as well. 

There’s then the following DLC exclusive cosmetics:

  • ADV Headset (multiple colorways)
  • Tactical Beanie (variant w/ Headset, multiple colorways)
  • Tactical Cap (Forward and Backward Variants, w/ 2x Comms Headset options and multiple colorways)
  • Bone Conduction Tactical Headset
  • Single-Ear Radio Headset (Black and OD Green)
  • Tactical Rebreather Gasmask
  • ‘Artemis’ Heavy Tactical Vest Cosmetic Variant (multiple colorways)
  • Machinex Fingerless Gloves
  • 4 DLC Exclusive Tattoos
  • Chameleon Boots
  • 2 more Tattoos (available after fully completing Boiling Point missions)
  • 2 more Cosmetic Items (available after fully completing Boiling Point missions)

Alongside the Boiling Point DLC, a free update adds new weapons to the arsenal, regardless of DLC ownership. This include the G18-C Pistol (which is capable of full-auto fire), S2011-P Pistol, and RTWC-6.5 Battle Rifle. New attachments then include the RMO Sight w/ 3x Magnification and a multi laser/IR/Flashlight combo.

Ready or Not is the hit tactical SWAT shooter, which hit version 1.0 in 2023 after several years in Steam Early Access, before jumping to consoles last year. In our review for the console launch, Miguel said, “Ready Or Not is an incredible co-op experiencing, blending tough-as-nails encounters with hilarious co-op shenanigans and dynamic objectives. It isn’t nearly as much fun singleplayer, but it’s definitely just as tense.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/12/ready-or-not-boiling-point-dlc-is-out-now-with-three-new-maps/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Directive 8020’s new trailer is all about the ‘movie night’ multiplayer
item.content:encoded:
Supermassive Games has dipped a toe into live-action trailers to tout the Movie Night Mode in their upcoming sci-fi horror game Directive 8020 – launching on 12th May 2026 for PC, PS5 and Xbox Series X|

Movie Night Mode in Directive 8020 allows up to five players to go through the story together, assigning each player the control of a particular crew member from the Cassiopeia, passing the controller back and forth as characters swap in the story.

At launch this will only be for the local multiplayer, but five-player online multiplayer will also be available via a free post-launch update.

However you play, together or alone, Directive 8020 marks the start of an ambitious new era for Supermassive’s horror games. This is a sci-fi adventure, and it’s a more direct action game than before, in many ways. The game will star Hollywood actor Lashana Lynch who you may have caught in Bob Marley: One Love or The Woman King.

A new feature for the series is Turning Points, which allows you to more easily rewind and revisit key moments in the story. This will allow for completionists to explore narrative branches, or for regret-filled players to go back and undo fatal mistakes, and it applies to Movie Night as well, allowing the group to rewind and try to redo something that went wrong. Alternatively, you can play in Survival Mode for that more traditional narrative adventure feel where all decisions are final.

In group sessions and Movie Night mode, Turning Points adds an extra layer of social tension and excitement. If a favourite character dies, players can collectively debate over whether to accept the consequence… or rewind and attempt to save them, ensuring no two group playthroughs are ever the same! For groups who want to live and die by their choices, Survival Mode locks in every consequence, where all decisions are final.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/12/directive-8020s-new-trailer-is-all-about-the-movie-night-multiplayer/

item.title: How the Devs of ‘Stupid Never Dies’ Captured the Right Amount of 'Funky Zombie Action'
item.content:encoded:
It's easy to get overwhelmed by the number of reveals shown during The Game Awards, but one of the few announcements that resonated with me was this punk-rock, flashy, and colorful music video featuring a zombie and his crush. I'm talking about Stupid Never Dies, created by a brand new studio. GPTRACK50 is the name of said studio, and though it's a new name, the developers behind it are anything but. Headed by President Hiroyuki Kobayashi, a 27-year Capcom veteran, he's supported by developers who've worked on Resident Evil, Devil May Cry, Monster Hunter, Metal Gear Solid, and more.

During our hands-off preview, TechRaptor was given a presentation covering the combat system of Stupid Never Dies: this is referred to as "Funky Zombie Action." After, we had a chance to conduct a Q&A style email interview with Kobayashi-san, so be sure to check out our talk further in the article!

Funky Zombie Action Creates a Dynamic Combat System in Stupid Never Dies

Believe me when I say "Funky Zombie Action" fits Stupid Never Dies to a T. The super stylized, pop-punk aesthetic is the perfect fit for an action game, creating a dazzling display of color and chaos. Said chaos is brought about by Stupid Never Dies' many monsters, of which our protagonist, Davy, can take the form of.

In the world of Stupid Never Dies, zombies are at the bottom of the monster hierarchy. Even so, this doesn't stop Davy from jumping into the fray to save the girl he loves. After stumbling across a frozen-solid damsel in distress (Julia), he falls in love and sets out to resurrect her. To resurrect Julia, Davy needs to defeat the King of Monsters (KOM).

The standard gameplay loop revolves around runs in a time-based dungeon filled to the brim with monsters to crush. By himself, Davy is relatively weak (he is, after all, a zombie), but with those newfound powers, he can consume and steal abilities from other monsters. 

In doing so, he becomes that very monster in combat, which allows players to dynamically switch between forms on the fly. There are 11 total forms called "styles," and each style appears to drastically change Davy's move set. To obtain a style, Davy first needs to perform a "Stupid Parry," which exposes a monster's core. Davy can then consume the core and transform into that monster.

This is an exciting system, if anything because of the design of each style. During the presentation, each one seemed to get more and more ridiculous and fun, and the variety here is pretty impressive. Of the 11 different styles, we have: Zombie, Werewolf, Harpy, Golem, Vampire, Will-o'-the-Wisp, Cyclops, Snow Fairy, Merfolk, Lich, and Demon. One neat detail about styles is that each one still retains some elements of Davy's punk outfit.

GPTRACK50 President Hiroyuki Kobayashi's favorite style is the werewolf. This is a style that focuses more on speed, with large swipes from the werewolf's claws. The werewolf can even conjure up a whirlwind-type flurry of blows, so it seems perfect for taking on larger groups of enemies.

Conversely, we have the harpy. The gameplay clip showed off the ability to fly and shoot feathers from a longer distance, and seemed slower -- but no less useful -- compared to the werewolf. 

Some of the other styles are self-explanatory, with the golem being more tanky and the cyclops having larger, sweeping, hard-hitting blows. Some styles appear more technical than others, such as the Will-o'-the-Wisp, which switches from the physical to astral plane so as to avoid damage; meanwhile, the Snow Fairy utilizes a sweet-looking ice sword and can freeze enemies and "make their power yours."

It's hard to say how the Styles will feel in the hands of the player until we actually get to play it for ourselves. Switching between styles is quick and seamless, but our assumption is all 11 powers can't be used in a single run. 

Based on our interview below, we learned that more powerful versions of the Styles, with new and improved abilities, can be acquired later on in runs. Either way, GPTRACK50 is vague on details concerning actual runs through the dungeon and Stupid Never Dies' metaprogression, with promises to reveal more at a later date. 

Outside of styles, however, Stupid Never Dies features a "body hack" system which allows Davy to modify parts of his body with gear. Zombie style is the one we're going to use the most, so to add more flavor to Davy's toolkit, body hacking allows for a more diverse set of skills. 

Some examples include attaching a gravity launcher to his arm, which shoots a projectile that'll group enemies together. There's also the massive edge, which is this sword about the same size as Davy himself; by attaching it to his hand, he can sweep through large crowds of enemies.

And to emphasize the idea that Stupid Never Dies is all about "Funky Zombie Action," GPTRACK50 showcased "Davy Burst," which is essentially his special move. In an explosion of psychedelic color, Davy gets a power boost and we see a clip of him absolutely demolishing a group of enemies.

Of course, there's so much more to Stupid Never Dies that we have yet to see, including dungeon runs and how progression works, but what's showcased here is exciting. Really, it's the sheer variety of different forms that gives me a great deal of confidence that combat won't get stale, and that it's mechanically rich enough to appeal to even hardcore action gamers. That, combined with GPTRACK50 comprising a bunch of veteran developers, instills hope that Stupid Never Dies will be a excellent action title.

GPTRACK50 President Hiroyuki Kobayashi on Making Monsters in Stupid Never Dies

TechRaptor: There are so many different monsters you could choose to use for styles, so can you talk about your reasoning on why you chose some of the styles you did? (e.g., Vampires, Liches, Demons, Will-o’-the-Wisp, and more)

Hiroyuki Kobayashi: From the many monsters available, we selected ones that best support different player playstyles.

Within the team, we discussed and chose monsters that fit various combat approaches, such as close-range, mid-range, long-range, speed-focused, power-focused, flying, and even underwater types.

TR: Each style has their own set of moves, based on the design of the monster you’re taking the form of. Were there any specific action games that influenced the move set of certain monsters? What was your design philosophy for these moves?

HK: When designing each style, we looked at techniques from various action games, but our primary focus was on how to reflect the imagery and characteristics that gamers associate with those classic monsters.

In terms of the philosophy behind the move design, we wanted to fully embrace the protagonist’s identity as a zombie. The idea is essentially: “Even if you lose two or three arms, don’t worry about it -- forget defense and keep attacking!”

Our goal is to create funky, aggressive action gameplay where constantly pushing the attack feels powerful and delivers a strong sense of catharsis for the player.

TR: Zombie form is said to be the foundation of all other styles. Is the Zombie style able to hold its own in battle? How often are you using Zombie style compared to other forms Davy can take on?

HK: The Zombie Style is fully viable in combat. However, taking on stronger monsters will require a certain level of technique.

At the start of every dungeon, the player always begins in Zombie Style, and since it is also used in combination with other styles, it will likely be the style players use most frequently.

TR: How did you navigate the development of 11 total styles, assuring that each style fit their own niche and role in the combat of Stupid Never Dies?

HK: The Zombie Style was designed as a standard character that is easy to handle. In contrast, the other characters are meant to be extremely powerful when used in the right situations -- but much tougher to use if the conditions don’t fit.

To achieve this balance, we first considered the types of pressure a player might face from individual enemies, as well as the situations that could arise from different enemy combinations. Based on that, we determined what kinds of situations each style should excel in.

From there, we designed each style’s actions, attack range, movement speed, attack speed and area, and even character size to ensure they could effectively break through those specific situations.

TR: From a gameplay perspective, were any styles more challenging than others to develop a cohesive combat style for? Can you explain what hurdles you faced in developing so many diverse styles for Davy to take the form of?

HK: The Harpy Style and Will-o’-the-Wisp Style, which excel at long-range combat, were particularly challenging to design.

From the early stages of development, we had certain assumptions about the ideal number of enemies, the appropriate combat distance, and the right level of pressure during battles. However, as development progressed, many of these elements only became clear through the process itself.

TR: Stupid Never Dies is fast-paced and heavy on the action, and you can switch between different styles to fit your needs in combat. How are you making it so that switching between styles doesn’t break the flow of battle?

HK: With the exception of clearly committed moments, players are allowed to cancel most actions by switching styles. We also designed the style-change action to be quick and fluid so that it does not disrupt the overall tempo of the game.

During development, we experimented with more elaborate visual transitions, but in the end we settled on the current instant-style switching, which felt the most responsive.

For example, players can use the Merfolk Style to dive and rapidly close the distance of an enemy, then immediately switch to Golem Style to deliver a powerful strike.

This kind of combat wouldn’t be possible using the Golem Style alone, since it moves very slowly.

By enabling instant style switching, we designed the system so that players can compensate for each style’s weaknesses while amplifying their strengths, allowing for a wide range of creative play combinations.

TR: What are some ways in which Stupid Never Dies encourages players to use different styles in combat, rather than sticking to using one the whole time?

HK: For example, even within the Werewolf Style, the skills you obtain will vary depending on which werewolf you Style Eat.

If you Style Eat a stronger werewolf found in the deeper layers of the dungeon, you can acquire a more powerful version of the Werewolf Style.

We encourage players to switch styles actively, choosing the one that best matches their strategy, the skills that complement their current deck, or the style that best suits the immediate combat situation.

TR: Davy himself can be upgraded with various body hacks, but are there ways that we can also enhance the abilities of the different styles in Stupid Never Dies?

HK: The game features both temporary progression and permanent progression systems.

Temporary progression includes elements such as selecting rarities that suit your current style during a run.

As for the permanent progression systems, please stay tuned for upcoming details.

TR: I also want to touch on The Game Awards reveal. As a new studio, what were your thoughts leading up to the reveal and do you have any comments on the reception for the trailer? 

HK: Right before the announcement, we were very nervous. Watching it actually be revealed at The Game Awards, our main feeling was, “We finally did it -- GPTRACK50’s first title has been announced.” It has been a long journey to get to this point.

Thanks to the support of the media, we feel the trailer was successfully shared with audiences around the world. We were especially surprised -- and very happy -- to see such a strong response from South America and Central Asia.

TR: I actually listened to the trailer on repeat, because I really enjoyed the pop/punk-style song. I’m curious about why you chose to go with a song like this and how it is representative of the game itself? Can we expect more music like this in the final product?

HK: Many action games tend to feature rock-style music, so when creating a new IP, we wanted to challenge ourselves by exploring a different musical direction.

When thinking about the brand identity of this title, we came up with several keywords based on the game’s themes: emo, street culture, speed, youth, and recklessness. From there, we decided that the music should lean toward “pop punk.”

TR: Is there anything else you would like people to know about Stupid Never Dies that hasn't been asked?

HK: With a brand-new title, it’s incredibly challenging to first get people to know about it and then get them to actually play it. We would be truly grateful if action game fans and members of the media could support this title, Stupid Never Dies, and help us spread the word.

Thank you very much for your support!!



https://techraptor.net/gaming/interviews/how-devs-behind-stupid-never-dies-injected-just-right-amount-of-funky-zombie
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hazard Pay is a block-pushing puzzle game you can really sink your mop into
item.content:encoded:
Video games are such funny things, because I think if you were to tell someone unfamiliar with them that there is genuine enjoyment to be had in pushing some blocks around in a virtual space they'd look at you as if you'd told them there's genuine enjoyment to be hand in pushing some blocks around in a virtual space. And yet it's true! It's one of the classic puzzle game genres, going way back to 1982 with Sokoban. And here we are now, many years later, with a new entry to the genre in the form of Hazard Pay, a dystopian take that sees you cleaning up mysterious messes in secret labs.

These messes that you have to clean up come in various forms. For example, there might be a patch of hazardous material that you have to guide towards a vat of acid. Moving around that material will spread more waste (blood) on the floor, however, and so you need to use your tools (a mop and bucket) to clean up after yourself, preferably using them to move the bits and bobs towards the vat to clean as you go. Tools like your mop and bucket and the hazardous material can be touched no problem, and can move one another, but you can't touch the vat directly, unless you put a lid on it, which there may not be in every level.

This gets ramped up in difficulty in various ways, like an annoyingly placed chair, or purposefully awkwardly laid out levels that mean you really have to think about how you're navigating the space. It also mixes things up by presenting you with just loose shirts and underwear that you can sort of fuse together to mop up the aforementioned blood. Amongst all of this, you also have to make sure you load up any waste onto your truck, as well as your tools if you have them, so it's not enough to just clean up after yourself.

It's a neat little puzzle game that is a lot more show than it is tell, with little tutorialisation shoved down your throat unnecessarily. Though, perhaps a little bit of an obvious nudge that you can hold down R to reset a level quickly would have saved me having to exit and re-enter the level more times than I'd like to admit.

Still, if any of this sounds up your alley, the demo is there for you to try out on Steam too, which you can check out right here.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/hazard-pay-is-a-block-pushing-puzzle-game-you-can-really-sink-your-mop-into
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Anomaly President launches in Early Access on August 3
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Fast-paced roguelike action game dated.

Side-scrolling roguelike action game Anomaly President will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam on August 3, developer Phew Phew Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Anomaly President is here with its unique presidential roguelike gameplay where you can destroy your enemies in the craziest ways!

Take on the mission to overthrow a president pushing the world toward disaster. But fighting is not enough. To save the world, you must also run for presidency.

Deadly Warrior by Night, Presidential Candidate by Day

Lead a double life in a world of chaos and corruption. By day, you’re a presidential candidate, winning the hearts and minds of the people. By night, you transform into a vigilante warrior, using your weapons to take down the criminal gangs controlled by the current president.

In a world filled with anomalies, you will encounter various enemies and vice president bosses, each with their own unique anomaly power. For example, did you know the president’s PR chief has the power of HEART, making people fall in love with them?! The good news is, by defeating these bosses, you can acquire their anomaly powers!

You Can’t Become President by Yourself; You Need a Team

You need a trustworthy team to run campaigns and empower you on your path to presidency. Choosing the right team is crucial in Anomaly President! They’ll be the ones to help you win the race!

You Need a Base on Your Journey to Presidency

To combat hidden crime syndicates and also run your presidential campaign simultaneously, you need a base that moves with you. Enter the campaign bus!

Design your campaign bus to suit your style and needs! Start from scratch, add unique sections, and create a personalized mobile base for your campaign.

Choose Your Weapon and Anomaly Power!

Special anomaly upgrades you’ll encounter while fighting will shape your warrior identity. You can become a sword master who parries enemies at the right time or transform into a cyber mage dealing mass damage with anomaly powers. In Anomaly President, the choices are plenty!

The best part is, the roguelike structure lets you experiment with all options. Try out different powers and progress with the one that best suits your style! There are many ways to evolve in Anomaly President.

Anomaly President Wants You!

Travel the country, explore new horizons, win the people’s hearts, destroy the current president’s gangs, liberate the people from tyranny, and ultimately get your revenge!

Anomaly President is calling you to action!

Watch a new trailer below.

Early Access Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/anomaly-president-launches-in-early-access-on-august-3
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: First-person puzzle platformer Yerba Buena announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Starring an NPC in an abandoned gameworld.

Publisher Focus Entertainment and developer Mad About Pandas have announced Yerba Buena, a first-person puzzle platformer played from the perspective of an NPC in an abandoned gameworld. It will be available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Focus Entertainment:

About

The world is a game. Break the rules.

Step into a surreal puzzle platforming adventure as Barb, a young woman living as an NPC in an abandoned gameworld. When a sinister plot threatens your beloved San Francisco, you must step up to save it with the help of a mysterious and powerful device: the Oscillator.

Copy the movement and physical traits of the world around you, reapply them to manipulate the environment and solve inventive puzzles: give a table the bounce of a trampoline, send entire buildings across city blocks or turn solid walls into thin air!

Track down a dangerous biker gang and uncover the truth behind the glitch that threatens your world.

Key Features

  • Dive into a unique video game world set in 1970’s San Francisco and save your city with the help of your friends and some unlikely allies.
  • Master a ground-breaking “Copy and Paste” mechanic that lets you apply the movement and physical traits of objects to manipulate the gameworld.
  • Unlock new Oscillator features as you go, and combine them to solve mind-bending environmental puzzles!
  • Discover the origins of the mysterious glitch that threatens your city, and become the main character of your own story.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/first-person-puzzle-platformer-yerba-buena-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Jeff Kaplan Reveals His Breaking Point at Blizzard: It Was the Biggest ‘F You’
item.content:encoded:
Jeff Kaplan has explained what his breaking point was while working at Blizzard.

Jeff Kaplan recently participated in a lengthy interview on the Lex Fridman Podcast. During the discussion, he explained that his breaking point at Blizzard came during a conversation with the company’s CFO about Overwatch’s success.

Jeff Kaplan Had the Fate of 1,000 People in His Hands

Kaplan recalls being told several years ago that the game had to make a certain amount of money. The exact amount was bleeped out out of respect for his confidentiality agreement with Blizzard. There was also a recurring Revenue amount that was bleeped out. He was also told that if those metrics weren’t met, they would lay off 1,000 people and that it would be on Kaplan’s head.

Kaplan, who did end up leaving Activision Blizzard in 2021, was a co-creator of Overwatch and called that moment the biggest “fuck you” of his career. He would also say that the executive is no longer working at the company. Dennis Durkin, although unnamed, was the CFO of Activision Blizzard at the time that Kaplan is referring to.

Overwatch’s Recent Changes

After re-releasing as a sequel in 2023, Overwatch 2 reverted to its original name and is now taking a different narrative-driven approach. Blizzard has insisted that the name change does not mean that the original switch was a failure. Learn more about the change here.

Right now, the gaming industry is undergoing massive restructures in big and small companies. Perhaps most notable was Highguard, a highly touted game that had its servers go offline less than 2 months after starting. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about this interaction? Do you think that is a justifiable breaking point? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/jeff-kaplan-reveals-his-breaking-point-at-blizzard-it-was-the-biggest-f-you/

item.title: Remothered: Red Nun’s Legacy announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher and developer Stormind Games, along with Soft Source Publishing, have announced Remothered: Red Nun’s Legacy, the third entry in the Remothered series following Remothered: Tormented Fathers and Remothered: Broken Porcelain. It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via Stormind Games:

About

Sometimes, seeking the truth means losing yourself.

Thirteen months after six young girls vanished in Sicily, a new clue reopens the nightmare.

When a mysterious recording rekindles the hope that her daughter might still be alive, Susan, against all reason, decides to go to the place where everything seems to have begun: a decaying noble residence isolated on the slopes of Mount Etna and once owned by the Ashmann family.

What starts as a desperate search soon becomes a journey into terror, where family secrets, memory, and obsession intertwine. Susan finds herself drawn into a labyrinth of relentless stalkers, hidden symbols, and supernatural phenomena. As reality fractures and dark powers rise, every step brings her closer to the truth… and to a terror older than herself.

Created to captivate both newcomers and longtime fans of the Remothered saga, Red Nun’s Legacy blends classic survival horror, stealth, and psychological horror, introducing new mechanics centered on perception, hypnosis, and environmental manipulation.

Key Features

  • Survive Horrific Stalkers – Challenge your survival instinct to get away from murderous enemies, hide and evade the deadly stalkers hunting you down, and fight back in critical moments.
  • A Homage to Old-School Horror – Feel the atmosphere of this heartfelt love letter to 3rd person survival horrors of the past.
  • Hypnosis Powers – Read objects to reveal forgotten memories, expose concealed passages, and reshape your understanding of the environment.
  • Explore a Richly Detailed Environment – Find clues and key items, and solve puzzles to access new areas and progress to the final revelation.
  • Uncover the Secrets of the Cristo Morente – Learn the forgotten story of the convent, the cradle where the myth of the Red Nun began.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/remothered-red-nuns-legacy-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Aphelion launches April 28
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Physical edition due out on July 2.

Cinematic science-fiction third-person action adventure game Aphelion will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on April 28, developer DON’T NOD announced. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Aphelion will cost $29.99 / €29.99 on PC and $34.99 / €34.99 on consoles. Xbox Series and Steam users will also be able to take advantage of a 10 percent discount during the launch window.

A physical edition, dubbed the “Pioneer Edition,” will launch for PlayStation 5 on July 2, via distributor Maximum Entertainment.

Here is an overview of the game, via DON’T NOD:

About

Stranded on a frozen planet, astronaut Ariane must brave rugged landscapes and shifting realities to rescue her wounded partner, Thomas. This action-packed science-fiction adventure blends exploration, traversal, and tense stealth gameplay.

A Science-Fiction Action Adventure on the Edge of Our Solar System

By 2060, Earth will be uninhabitable. The discovery of a ninth planet—Persephone—at the edge of the solar system, is humanity’s best hope. The European Space Agency is dispatching the Hope 01 scientific mission, consisting of two of their accomplished astronauts, Ariane and Thomas. Their mission: to survey the planet and determine whether humanity can prosper anew here.

Your adventure begins in chaos, as the crash landing of their ship scatters them across the planet, launching them on a desperate quest to find one another.

Aphelion is a cinematic third-person action-adventure set on the planet Persephone, an uncharted, frozen world. Stranded and isolated, you must survive the truly unknown, find each other, and complete the mission. Traverse stunning alien landscapes, avoid lurking threats, and discover the haunting secrets buried beneath the ice.

Explore an Uncharted Planet

Explore the surreal beauty of Persephone, a vast frozen world shaped by deadly weather and ever-changing terrain.

Armed only with your explorational tools—pathfinder, oxygen tank, grappling hook, and more—you must face the unknown, traverse treacherous terrain, and gather clues about the strange, reality-bending phenomenon threatening your existence.

Find Each Other

Helpless on the edge of the solar system, the Hope 01 crew faces a brutal new world and the echoes of a complicated past. Ariane Montclair and Thomas Cross share a deep connection, a relationship left unresolved by the demands of their careers and their mission. Facing overwhelming odds, their fractured bond becomes a lifeline as they grasp in the dark for one another.

Hide to Survive

You’re not alone. Something lurks beneath the ice.

Key Features

  • A realistic depiction of space exploration.
  • An emotional drama on an alien world.
  • Cinematic action-adventure with gripping traversal gameplay.
  • Tools-based exploration and survival.
  • Tense and exciting stealth-based sequences.

Watch a new trailer below.

Persephone Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/aphelion-launches-april-28
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The 7th Guest Remake announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Vertigo Games has announced The 7th Guest Remake for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC (Steam, Epic Games Store). It will launch in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

The iconic game The 7th Guest has been brought back to life with modern technology, delivering an atmospheric story like no other.

Six guests have been welcomed to a foreboding mansion, but something sinister is at play. The wealthy recluse and toymaker Henry Stauf lurks in the shadows, and a dark power hangs over the house, shrouded in mystery. Who is the seventh guest? What does Henry want with them? And who will live to tell the tale?

As you explore the eerie mansion, the puzzles grow increasingly challenging, with dangers lurking around every corner. Every shadow, distant creak, and flicker of light builds tension as the house slowly reveals its secrets.

The 7th Guest is the ultimate mystery adventure for players who love mind-bending puzzles, haunting exploration, and a chilling story.

Key Features

  • Cinematic Storytelling Through Volumetric Video – Step inside the story. Using cutting-edge, newly recorded volumetric video, live-action performances are captured in full 3D, placing you directly inside Henry Stauf’s mansion at the heart of the mystery. Real actors appear within the world itself, making every encounter feel immediate and unsettling.
  • Unique Mind-Bending Puzzle Gameplay – The equally beloved and feared haunted mansion comes alive around you with high-end visuals and carefully redesigned, all-new handcrafted puzzles. Every puzzle is woven into the story and rooted in the mansion’s lore, with recognizable elements that pay homage to the original.
  • Dynamic Environments – The haunted mansion shifts and transforms around you through high-end visuals and clever optical illusions. Investigate every creepy nook and cranny. Unlock new rooms, uncover hidden secrets, and keep your wits about you as the horrors within begin to close in.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/the-7th-guest-remake-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-and-pc

item.title: PEGI revises its age ratings system with considerations for loot boxes, daily quests
item.content:encoded:
European video game ratings organization Pan-European Game Information, better known as PEGI, is revising its age ratings system to tie  to specific features in games, like loot boxes and daily quests. PEGI announced the changes on Thursday, noting that the new classifications will go into effect in June.

"Newly submitted games will be classified with a broader set of criteria that will focus on content and functionality, such as purchases of in-game content, paid random items, communication features, and features that incentivize players to continue playing," it said in the announcement. PEGI added that the change will "help bolster online safety and meet the concerns and questions of today's parents."

Here are the new categories, from PEGI's announcement:

  • Purchases of in-game content: games with time-limited or quantity-limited offers will be classified with a PEGI 12, games with NFTs or blockchain-related mechanisms will be PEGI 18.

  • Paid random items: the default rating will be PEGI 16 if the game contains paid random items (and in some cases they can be PEGI 18).

  • Play-by-appointment: mechanisms that reward returning to the game (e.g. daily quests) will get a PEGI 7. If these mechanisms punish players for not returning (e.g. by losing content or reducing progress) they will become PEGI 12.

  • Safe online gameplay: if games contain entirely unrestricted communication features (e.g. no blocking or reporting), they will be PEGI 18.

Related:Titanium Court wins Seumas McNally Grand Prize at 28th annual IGF awards

To put it simply, games that include loot boxes will now be rated at PEGI 16, and, in some cases, PEGI 18. This means that games in the EA Sports FC franchise, which have a PEGI 3 rating, could see a dramatic age upgrade for future versions, due to the controversial loot boxes in those games. EA no longer sells loot boxes in EA FC 26 in South Korea due to laws that would require the company to disclose detailed probability data.

PEGI did not expand on what would bump up the rating to PEGI 18. Game Developer has reached out for clarification. Anything that sells items on a limited basis—time or quantity—will be rated at PEGI 12. For example, something using a rotating store like Fortnite's would likely be rated at PEGI 12. The play-by-appointment rating targets games that incentivize a player coming back daily to unlock things or earn rewards, ranking those at PEGI 7. Anything that punishes players for not coming back would be PEGI 12. The last category rates games with "unrestricted communication" as PEGI 18.

"Games can be submitted to PEGI for examination in advance of their announcement and their release, therefore PEGI expects that the first games classified under these new criteria will be announced later in summer of this year," PEGI said. Games are often submitted to PEGI before they're announced, so the games that are announced this summer are most likely to have considered the new PEGI ratings classifications.

Related:Pokemon Pokopia has topped 2.2M sales in four days

Loot boxes, in particular, are a big deal in the industry. Regulations have targeted them for years, and, most recently, New York Attorney General Letitia James sued Steam maker Valve Corporation over loot boxes in Counter-Strike 2, Team Fortress 2, and Dota 2. "The lawsuit alleges that Valve has made billions of dollars luring its users, many of whom are teenagers or younger, to engage in gambling in the hopes of winning expensive virtual items that they can cash in on," the Attorney General's office wrote in a news release. Valve responded with a news release of its own, saying that loot boxes like theirs are common in and and elsewhere, comparing them to Pokémon cards and Labubu.
Activision Blizzard was fined a meager €5,000 by PEGI in 2023 for not disclosing loot boxes in Diablo Immortal.

About the Author

Columnist, Game Developer

Nicole Carpenter is a veteran reporter who has been covering the video game industry for over a decade. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/pegi-revises-its-age-ratings-system-with-considerations-for-loot-boxes-daily-quests

item.title: Play You Are Elon Musk, The Sequel To You Are Jeff Bezos
item.content:encoded:
'Tank your reputation or impress the internet masses. Unionize Tesla. Get a vasectomy'

Published: March 12, 2026, 5:07 pm

Eight years after the release of You Are Jeff Bezos--a text adventure where you, a normal person, must try to spend the fortune of one of the richest and most terrible men on the planet--creator Kris Lorischild has blessed us with You Are Elon Musk, a text adventure where you, a normal person, must try to spend the fortune of one of the richest and even more terrible men on the planet.

Placing you body swap-style inside Musk for a day and asking you a succession of branching, multiple-choice questions, the game features:

The long-awaited sequel to You Are Jeff Bezos, exploring the age-old question: What would you do if you had more money than any single human being should ever have?
Give to important causes or feed your own vanity. Tank your reputation or impress the internet masses. Unionize Tesla. Get a vasectomy. All this and more in this hot, thrilling new Twine game with 200 passages and 7 endings!

I chose to spend the money ending poverty, eliminating lunch debt, funding artists and buying a superyacht (as a treat, for myself), before a mishap involving Steve Bannon and a giant robot sent me careening to my death. How your day as Musk will end depends on the choices you make. Good luck, and if you dig the game, Kris' Patreon page is here.



https://aftermath.site/elon-musk-video-game/

item.title: “It Broke Me” – Overwatch Creator Finally Reveals the Truth Behind the Painful Separation from Blizzard
item.content:encoded:
After several years of silence, Overwatch creator Jeff Kaplan has resurfaced to announce his new game, the action survival multiplayer FPS The Legend of California. At the same time, he was also interviewed by Lex Fridman in a massive, multi-hour interview where he revealed the truth behind his exit from Blizzard after 19 years at the company, during which he also worked as a tester on Warcraft III: Reign of Chaos and Game Director on World of Warcraft. Kaplan described the separation as very painful:

It broke me. I think after you've been at a place like Blizzard, which I love Blizzard to this day, I have nothing but warm, fond memories. I mean, there's those moments where you're like, I wish that hadn't happened. But on the whole, that place is a Mecca for game development. Everything I have is due to Blizzard. They provided for my family and me, made me the person I am. So, separating from Blizzard was one of the most painful things. And I was very sad when I resigned and I didn't realize how broken I was until recently. Like the mourning grieving I had gone through of like, I think I'm a little [ __ ] in the head for not being there any How could I give that up? How could I not be there anymore? It was really, really painful leaving.

That pain is reflected in Kaplan's new studio's name: Kintsugiyama, a portmanteau of kintsugi (the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery with gold) and yama, meaning "mountain." Kaplan said he (and other former Blizzard employees who have joined) didn't come away unscarred, and that's beautiful in its own way.

But why did he leave Blizzard in April 2021? Well, he talked about that, too. At some point, Activision Blizzard's Chief Financial Officer pressured him to achieve a specific annual revenue target for Overwatch, threatening to lay off 1,000 employees if he didn't. That was Kaplan's wake-up call.

What it boiled down for me, like what sort of ultimately broke me in my Blizzard career was I got called in the CFO's office and he sits me down and he gives me a date (which at the time was 2020, and was going to slip to 2021, but at the time it was 2020) and he said 'Overwatch has to make [X amount] in 2020, and then every year after that it needs a recurring revenue.' And then he says to me, 'If it doesn't do [X], we're going to lay off a thousand people, and that's going to be on you.'

And that was just the biggest [fuck you] moment I had in my career. It felt surreal to be in that condition. As somebody who's worked on a lot of games, made a lot of games, and you get in these meetings where they're like, 'Fortnite has 1,400 people working on it, if you just hire 1,400 people and make it free to play, we'll make that money, right?' And that was… I had believed I would never work any place but Blizzard. I loved it. It was a part of who I was. And I felt I was a part of it. And I literally thought I would retire from a place. I never thought the day would come, but that was it. Luckily for Blizzard, that CFO is no longer there.

Kaplan does not name the CFO, but before Kaplan resigned, that role belonged to Dennis Durkin, who left the company in May 2021, just a month after the game designer. Still, that was just the waterdrop that broke the dam. In the Fridman interview, Kaplan discussed other ongoing issues:

In 2016 and 2017, I felt very in control of the Overwatch team and the direction of the game as a game director, you know, working with Ray Gresco as the production director. It felt like we were running Overwatch, and we were very, very successful and doing a good job, and I think the fans were happy. And then as we transitioned, you know, Overwatch League was the best intention. My parents always say the road to hell is paved with good intentions. That was the Overwatch League. And it ended up being an albatross. And then Overwatch 2 was the same thing.

Jeff Kaplan helped pitch the competitive Overwatch League. The original vision was genuinely intriguing: regional franchise teams with player salary protections, a structure partly inspired by the faction-based loyalty of games like Dark Age of Camelot. The idea was that players would root for their city, as they do for their football club.

The problem started on the road show. Team ownership slots were sold to billionaire investors with projections that effectively promised NFL-level returns. The numbers were aspirational at best; when COVID made international live events impossible (London vs Shanghai isn't a local derby you can run in a bubble), the entire economic foundation collapsed, and Blizzard defaulted to the only remaining revenue lever: squeezing the live game.

That's when the walls started closing in on the development team. Team owners, having paid enormous sums for their stakes, began showing up with design opinions, and Kaplan says when that happened, he knew the devs were in trouble. Simultaneously, a massive Twitch streaming deal landed a cascade of contractual in-game technical obligations on engineering. Every sprint, every development cycle, was consumed by OWL features and Twitch deliverables. The game Kaplan actually wanted to make, the PVE story-driven Overwatch 2 that was announced at BlizzCon 2019, was perpetually deferred.

It became, in his words, a house of cards waiting to fall. And when it did, it also took Overwatch 2's soul. The PVE mode that was supposed to be the game's centrepiece, the thing that would have justified the sequel's existence, was gutted by the slow suffocation of a sports franchise business model grafted onto a live-service game that could never support the weight. Indeed, as you might recall, the cancellation of the PVE mode was hugely controversial in the community. Eventually, some PvE missions were released in 2023, but it was just a skeleton of what was promised.

As you'd expect, Kaplan didn't spend the whole time just talking about Overwatch. He also dropped more details about his new game, set in a fictional, alternate-history version of California reimagined as an island. Kaplan describes it as a deliberate departure from Blizzard's hero factory ethos: edgier in tone, lonelier, and mysterious. The landscape is handcrafted, voxel-based (every rock, hillside, and mine shaft is destructible and diggable), and procedurally seeded, so points of interest shift with every server. Dread Rock, a POI inspired by Alcatraz, might be sitting in San Francisco Bay on one server and out in the Mojave Desert on another.

Difficulty works on four tiers rather than character levels, and those tiers reshuffle with every seed, too, so no two servers share the same geography of danger. The world also resets periodically (currently, the team is thinking about a monthly reset), borrowing the mechanic from Rust, a game Kaplan has logged over 5,000 hours in. The explicit goal, however, is to make players excited about the wipe, not angry about it.

We'll definitely keep track of The Legend of California as it approaches its early access debut on PC later in 2026.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/jeff-kaplan-blizzard-cfo-threat-overwatch-revenue-resignation/

item.title: Battle Royale Games Took a Big Dive in 2025 Despite the Same Legacy Live Service Franchises Dominating Playtime
item.content:encoded:
We may be halfway into March 2026, but we're still in that time of the year when data firms and analysts are publishing their recaps of the previous year in the video game industry and reports for what's ahead. Global analytics firm Newzoo is the latest one to arrive and has published its PC and Console Gaming 2026 Report, which shows a few things we've seen before in other reports, as well as some new insights around some of the most popular genres in video games, like battle royales.

Firstly, some similarities. Newzoo's report, like Epyllion's, points out that PC gaming is pulling ahead of console gaming, with Newzoo adding that while PC gaming is driving more growth, console players are still spending more on average per individual. Overall, Newzoo predicts that PC gaming will continue to drive growth and outpace consoles into 2028, pushing the industry towards $103.7 billion in revenue.

Steam is also, unsurprisingly, the platform driving the bus on PCs, while for consoles, Newzoo cites the strength of first-party titles and higher subscription pricing as growth drivers, while also noting that the emphasis on subscriptions and a cycle of waiting for major releases is what keeps player growth down.

As for what people are spending their time playing, this is where things get particularly interesting. To start, the top 20 gaming franchises for 2025 according to Newzoo are exactly the ones you'd expect. They are, in the following order:

  • Roblox
  • Fortnite
  • Call of Duty
  • Minecraft
  • EA Sports FC
  • Counter-Strike 2
  • League of Legends
  • Grand Theft Auto V
  • NBA 2K
  • Marvel
  • World of Warcraft
  • Valorant
  • Dota 2
  • Rainbow Six
  • Battlefield
  • Apex Legends
  • Rocket League
  • Overwatch
  • The Sims 4
  • Monster Hunter

All recognizable franchises, some individual games, that are all live services, with 12 of them either entirely free-to-play or free to start with paid expansions and other paid elements. These games combined amounted to the majority of PC and console playtime across PC, PlayStation, and Xbox. That's even with franchises and games ranked out of the top 20 being the second-biggest representation of where players spend their time.

They also, unsurprisingly, make up most of the revenue, especially on consoles. Only on PC are players spending more on games outside of the top 20 most played games and franchises.

These 20 video games and franchises are without a doubt continuing to dominate the industry. Interestingly though, the genres that large portion of these games belong to, those being shooters and battle royales, saw a major decline in player engagement and playtime in 2025.

Playtime for battle royales dropped 27% in 2025 compared to 2024, while shooters too a not insignificant 5% dip in 2025 compared to 2024. Sandbox games, however, saw a major increase in 2025, rising 35% in playtime compared to 2024, with a lot of that driven by the success of Roblox.

Roblox's growth as a platform simply cannot be understated, and while this isn't a sign of the death of battle royale games or anything quite as drastic, it is a signifier for what we could see in the future. Will the youth of today spending all their time in Roblox transition to battle royales like Fortnite? Or will they stick to sandbox games and find something closer to what they grew up playing? The answer is likely to have a serious impact on what the industry will look like in the future.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/battle-royale-video-games-took-dive-in-engagement-despite-same-legacy-franchises-dominating/

item.title: Which Cowardly Game Company Will Be The First To Stand Up To Trump?
item.content:encoded:
Over the past few months, Kotaku has been covering the Trump administration’s continued use of pop culture to spread propaganda, whether that be using Pokémon to promote ICE or Call of Duty to “celebrate” America and Israel’s joint strikes on Iran. The more this keeps happening, the more I’m starting to wonder just how newsworthy it is, as Trump and his ilk thrive on the controversy and believe themselves untouchable because that’s how fascists operate. At the rate these vile videos are being put out by whatever social media intern the Trump administration has hired, I could be writing a new story about them every other day, especially now that the White House seems to almost be antagonizing Nintendo following its tariff lawsuit. Is anyone going to stand up to this administration, or are we just going to keep seeing every video game icon, movie hero, and popular cartoon character cheering on the Trump White House for the foreseeable future?

The latest video includes footage of the Iran strikes sandwiched between videos from Nintendo’s Wii Sports mini-games, complete with the game’s theme song playing over the footage. It’s a gross, dehumanizing shitpost paid for by American taxpayers, and Nintendo’s intellectual property is all over it. 

UNDEFEATED. pic.twitter.com/Jt69bcag5y

— The White House (@WhiteHouse) March 12, 2026

Technically, something like this could be protected under fair use, but an argument could also easily be made for trademark infringement and dilution, in that a video this reprehensible could be seen as brand-damaging to the IP it features. However, thus far most companies are either electing to ignore these videos or to just put out a statement clarifying that they weren’t involved in the making or distribution of this kind of political propaganda.

Nintendo is known for being especially litigious over far smaller offenses than using its characters and music to promote literal war crimes, though it has, at the very least, taken swings at the Trump administration for the money it lost due to the president’s tariffs which were deemed illegal by the Supreme Court in February.

It’s not like you need me to tell you that the way American politics has contorted itself to placate a government seemingly run by 12-year-old internet trolls isn’t normal, or that none of the usual systems that were supposed to keep checks and balances in place to stop a fascist regime seem to apply anymore. Everyone’s scared of a 79-year-old toddler because someone gave him a loaded gun that he’s swinging around. He gets to break laws, bypass protocols, lie, start wars, and endanger people in and outside of America on whatever destructive whims pop into his addled noggin.

Video games and other pieces of pop culture have been dragged into it because right wingers are notoriously incapable of creating anything themselves, and have to bastardize other people’s ideas so the masses will point and clap because they understood that reference. And since the rules don’t seem to actually apply and the Trump administration has a hell of a lot more resources to endure a legal battle than a guy who pirated some games does, no one’s taking any action here. At this point I don’t know if it’s cowardice, or if this idiocy has just become so normalized nobody even cares.

We’ve reached out to Nintendo for comment on this situation and will update the story if we hear back.



https://kotaku.com/white-house-video-wii-sports-iran-israel-nintendo-lawsuit-2000678455

item.title: WILL: Follow The Light Release Date Revealed by TomorrowHead
item.content:encoded:
TomorrowHead has unveiled the release date for WILL: Follow the Light, a stunning first-person narrative title built in UE5. In WILL: Follow the Light, players embark on an adventure, traversing inclement weather and conditions to get back to their family after a disaster strikes their hometown.

Players explore a ‘stunning northern wilderness’ and dig through a vast world in a bid to get home and discover what has happened after their son is discovered missing.

WILL: Follow The Light Coming April 28

TomorrowHead has confirmed that WILL: Follow the Light is coming to PlayStation 5, Xbox, and PC on April 28, 2026. This news was revealed, along with a new trailer, during today’s Future Games Show.

Speaking on the announcement, Roman Novikov, the Founder and Game Director at TomorrowHead, said:

We are excited to announce the imminent release of WILL: Follow the Light.

We hope our community and new players will be excited to see the game launch so soon, and we hope players will enjoy the game when it launches next month for PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox.

In WILL: Follow the Light, players step into the boots of Will, a lighthouse keeper on a remote island who has his reality shattered when a disaster hits home, and his son disappears. He’s forced to make his way home as quickly as possible, but the journey requires him to combat some extremely harsh terrain.

Players must solve puzzles, chart paths, and navigate by boat, dog sled, and on foot, all to reach home before it’s too late.

Does WILL: Follow the Light sound like your kind of game? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the new Crimson Desert launch trailer

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/will-follow-the-light-release-date/

item.title: WILL: Follow The Light launches April 28
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

First-person narrative adventure game dated.

First-person narrative adventure game WILL: Follow The Light will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on April 28, developer TomorrowHead Studio announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Dive into a realistic, single-player, first-person journey through the harsh northern latitudes as you sail endless waters, searching for a way back to your loved ones. Will you follow the light?

Dramatic Storyline

Uncover a narrative that will captivate you until the very end. WILL: Follow The Light, crafted by the young indie studio TomorrowHead, is an adventure story game that strives to push the boundaries of the genre. An escapism-themed plot takes inspiration from the game Firewatch, while the visuals and atmosphere are influenced by Alan Wake. WILL: Follow The Light features unusual but intuitive game mechanics against an enthralling backdrop of epic northern scenery. You will find yourself sailing between remote islands and uncharted waters.

Learn more about your own nature while exploring a variety of meaningful locations, from the rugged Nordic shores to the icy and stormy northern sea. The plot is filled with puzzles and mysterious elements related to your relatives, drawing you in and keeping you eager to unveil the next layer of the story until harsh reality drags you back. WILL‘s story revisits the timeless dilemma between fathers and sons and will thus reflect on you deeply. This emotional roller coaster will carry you through wild thunderstorms, the ringing silence of loneliness, and the importance of caring for your loved ones.

Key Features

  • Get ready to experience the thrill of managing a sailing yacht with realistic controls that were developed with input from actual voyagers’ stories, yet simplified for any player to enjoy. Navigating through cold and hostile environments has always been vital to northern people, and WILL, a fearless man who grew up on the sea, embodies this spirit of adventure.
  • Face challenges not only on the water, but also on land. Feel the endless contrast between two elements of nature as you take on the role of lightkeeper. To progress, you will solve puzzles rooted in real life, using an authentic weather station and lifelike navigation system. The beacon guides your way and illuminates your hopes.
  • Traverse treacherous, snowy trails with a dog sled and search for the answers. As you embark on this adventure, you will lose track of time, utterly transfixed by the mysterious ride.
    System Requirements

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/will-follow-the-light-launches-april-28
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Roguelite tower defense game ReVamp announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From Moonlighter developer Digital Sun.

Moonlighter series developer Digital Sun has announced ReVamp, a roguelite tower defense game coming to PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Rule as Dracula in this castle defense roguelite. Shape your fortress room by room, command an army of monsters, and step into battle yourself to protect your throne from relentless mortal invaders. A war waged for eternity, for the love you couldn’t save.

Rule as Dracula

You are Dracula. Not a monster in the shadows, but the sovereign of a castle under siege. Cursed by Achlys, goddess of the night, you are condemned to defend your throne without end. Mortals return night after night, and every siege becomes another chapter in an eternal war of blood and sacrifice. But you do not merely watch from afar. You move through your halls, strike down invaders with devastating abilities, and tip the balance of battle when your forces begin to falter. All for the love that was taken from you.

Build a Living Castle

The castle is yours to shape. Expand it room by room, reconfigure its structure between attacks, and transform open halls into calculated gauntlets. Each room alters the flow of battle, forcing invaders into choke points, layered defenses, and brutal confrontations. Invest the blood of fallen mortals to strengthen your fortress, empowering chambers, enhancing effects, and acquiring new units. With every placement and every upgrade, the labyrinth deepens, becoming less a structure… and more a weapon.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Crush the Invasion

Mortals march in waves, blinded by a foolish sense of justice. Armored knights, unyielding barbarians, or zealous priests, each deserving a punishment tailored to their arrogance. Your answer? An army of the night. Skeletal swordsmen and archers form disciplined ranks, while swarming rats flood narrow corridors and winged harpies strike from above.

Command the Night

When your legion wavers, step into the fray yourself. Move through the castle as Dracula and unleash devastating special abilities, and turn certain defeat into victory. The better you build, the more lethal you will be. Design your fortress with intention and exploit the shortcuts you carved into your own design.

You are not just the ruler of the castle. You are its final defense.

Endure the Eternal Loop

Each moonrise brings a new assault. Enemy formations shift, elite leaders emerge, and your defenses are tested in new ways. No two sieges unfold the same, and survival demands adaptation. When the castle falls, the night begins again. Yet defeat is not oblivion. The blood spilled in your halls becomes power. Between cycles, invest it to unlock new chambers, command stronger creatures, and access deeper layers of strategic possibility.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/roguelite-tower-defense-game-revamp-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Silver Pines ‘Gameplay’ trailer
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Mystery, action, and relentless horror.

Publisher Team17 and developer Wych Elm have released a gameplay trailer for Metroidvania / side-scrolling survival horror game Silver Pines.

Here is an overview of the game, via Team17:

About

Embark on an atmospheric and haunting journey, as you unearth unsettling revelations and battling nightmarish terrors while carefully managing your scarce resources to uncover mysteries that could hold the key to the most important case you will ever undertake. As the case to find vanished musician Eddie Velvet begins to reveal a dark past, Walker soon slips into a strange and nightmarish descent beyond his control.

Key Features

  • Investigate the Town of Silver Pines – Piece together the mystery through careful exploration, discovering clues and secrets hidden across the town.
  • Survival Horror Gameplay – A love letter to classic survival horror. With limited inventory space and scarce ammunition, every encounter becomes a tense decision between confrontation and escape.
  • Metroidvania Navigation – Encounter locked doors and hidden paths, an uncovered clue, a resource, or something you may not want to discover at all. As you find better tools and weapons, more of the town will become accessible.

Silver Pines is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC via Steam in 2026.

Watch the trailer below.

Gameplay Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/silver-pines-gameplay-trailer

item.title: Adorable Adventures launches April 30
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Cozy exploration game dated.

Adorable Adventures, an exploration game where you play as a curious baby boar seeking to reunite with his family, will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on April 30, publisher PQube and developer Wild Sheep Studio announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via PQube:

About

Adorable Adventures is a relaxing third-person exploration game where you play as Boris, a curious baby boar who must learn to use his nose to reunite with his family, who were separated after a forest fire.

Across rolling meadows, sun-dappled forest paths, deep caves, and rocky highlands, identify scents and follow trails to learn more about the world around you. Search for your brothers and sisters, uncover secrets, and expand your library of smells against the backdrop of a gentle and heart-warming story.

Key Features

  • A Boar’s Life – Play as a mischievous baby boar brimming with energy! Dash through open fields, root around dense undergrowth, scrabble over rocky ledges, and splash about in cool riverbeds.
  • Explore the Park – Traverse beautifully detailed environments inspired by the Cevennes National Park in Southern France. Take your time to enjoy its peaceful atmosphere and soothing natural sounds.
  • Follow Your Nose – Use Boris’s developing sense of smell to follow scents and identify their source. Filter the smells that you have learned to navigate the park and make exciting discoveries.
  • Reunite With Your Family – Search far and wide for your wayward brothers and sisters, and convince them to join in your adventure by solving puzzles that match their individual personalities.
  • Easily Distracted – Discover a collection of playful side activities. Compete in time trial races, master photography challenges, tidy up the park, and maybe even solve the mystery of a local legend?
  • A Heart-Warming Tale – Let Maxime, the park ranger, guide you through Boris’ journey of growth and self-discovery, featuring adaptive storytelling and full voiceover.
  • Reconnect With Nature – Track down and collect every scent in the park to unlock handwritten entries in Maxime’s journal, and decorate Boris with adorable accessories!

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/adorable-adventures-launches-april-30

item.title: Dave The Diver's Next Major DLC Launches This June
item.content:encoded:
Plus, a new physical edition is confirmed

Developer Mintrocket has confirmed that Dave the Diver's next major DLC, 'Into the Jungle', will launch on Switch and Switch 2 on 18th June 2026.

The expansion will feature entirely new locations, with additional NPCs, quests, and more. To put it simply, if you're a fan of the base game, then chances are you're going to love this one.

If that's not enough, it's also been confirmed that a new 'Complete Edition' physical release is inbound for Switch 2 and PS5. This includes the base game plus all DLC (yes, including 'Into the Jungle') on cartridge, and those who wish to splash out a bit more can also opt for the Collector's Edition.

This one will include the following:

  • Bancho Sushi Restaurant Acrylic Standee
  • 4 Metal Pin Badges
  • Acrylic Keyring
  • Dolphin Pendant Necklace
  • 2 Marinca Cards
  • Poster
  • 4 Postcards

Both editions can be pre-ordered from the Silver Lining Direct store. In the meantime, if you're on the fence about Dave the Dive, then be sure to check out our review. We gave it a score of 9/10 and said that it "rivals Mario Wonder's all-out density and charm while doing something almost completely new".

Will you be picking up Dave the Diver's upcoming DLC? How about the new physical edition? Let us know with a comment.

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply
